symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive vaginal dryness and rawness since menopause

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Genitourinary syndrome of menopause / vulvovaginal atrophy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive dryness after oestrogen withdrawal is the core symptom of urogenital atrophy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Genitourinary syndrome of menopause / vulvovaginal atrophy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Friable atrophic mucosa tears and bleeds on contact, causing dyspareunia with minor contact bleeding.
Postmenopausal bleeding of any amount must be examined in person to exclude a sinister cause before attributing it to atrophy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Genitourinary syndrome of menopause / vulvovaginal atrophy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Urine passing over micro-fissured atrophic urethral/introital tissue produces dysuria mimicking cystitis.
Dysuria and frequency are cardinal UTI symptoms — a genuine mimic requiring urine MCS to separate.

symptoms.symptom_name: History of recurrent culture-positive urinary tract infections

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Genitourinary syndrome of menopause / vulvovaginal atrophy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Atrophy (raised pH, loss of lactobacilli, urethral atrophy) predisposes to recurrent UTIs, so the two co-exist.
Prior recurrent UTIs raise baseline risk of a current infection, keeping active UTI on the differential.
Persistent symptoms despite repeated appropriate antifungal use argue against candidiasis as the cause.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Genitourinary syndrome of menopause / vulvovaginal atrophy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-response to antifungals is consistent with a non-infective atrophic aetiology, and the creams themselves aggravate the micro-trauma.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, systemically well, urine reported clear (not cloudy or foul-smelling)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, systemic upset and cloudy/foul urine lowers the likelihood of an active or complicated infection, though it does not exclude it without MCS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pale, thin, inelastic vaginal mucosa devoid of rugae with friable petechial bleeding-on-contact surfaces, labial retraction and a urethral caruncle

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Genitourinary syndrome of menopause / vulvovaginal atrophy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The classic atrophic examination signs — sealed clinician finding; the AI Doctor cannot inspect the genital tract and must route to in-person examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care urinalysis negative for nitrites and leukocytes

investigation_details.expected_result: Negative for nitrites and leukocytes; MCS to confirm no growth
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without urine testing the atrophic dysuria may be mistaken for infection and antibiotics given inappropriately.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative urinalysis makes active bacterial cystitis unlikely — sealed clinician/point-of-care result the AI cannot obtain remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Genitourinary syndrome of menopause / vulvovaginal atrophy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A raised vaginal pH reflects loss of oestrogen and lactobacilli in atrophy — sealed clinician measurement, not obtainable via chat.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Genitourinary syndrome of menopause / vulvovaginal atrophy
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Progressive dryness, dyspareunia with contact bleeding, atrophic dysuria plus the atrophic examination signs and raised vaginal pH together confirm urogenital atrophy — but the confirming elements are examination/investigation only.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person genital/speculum examination by a GP or urogynaecology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms atrophy and, critically, examines the source of postmenopausal contact bleeding to exclude a sinister cause — cannot be done via telehealth.
Separates atrophic dysuria from an active UTI before any antibiotic is considered.
Supportive of an atrophic diagnosis; performed at the in-person examination.
Dysuria here is atrophic (negative urinalysis, sealed); empirical antibiotics without supportive urine testing are inappropriate and must not be minted by the AI.
